summary: Betamethasone enhances fetal lung development reducing risks like RDS but does not definitively prevent hyperbilirubinemia; hence it’s an exception among listed benefits when administered pretermly during labor or before birth. The drug significantly reduces complications such as NRDS by accelerating surfactant production without preventing jaundice due to lack substantial proof linking its use directly against this condition. To be precise, prevention of hyperbilirubinemia stands out unsupported compared to others highlighted clinically beneficial effects from available data sources reviewed here today concerning maternal treatment timing strategies involving corticosteroids prior childbirth scenarios presented initially posed questions about potential therapeutic interventions aimed at optimizing newborn health
